key_insight: The paper turns nonlinear reaction--diffusion PDE reachability into a certifiable pipeline by combining FEM discretization, POD reduction, and set-based analysis with explicit uniform error bounds.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a practical way to over-approximate all possible PDE states under uncertainty, which is useful for safety verification and robust design of diffusion-driven systems.
method: Discretize the PDE with FEM to obtain a finite-dimensional surrogate.
Apply POD to build a reduced-order model from the FEM system.
Run reachability analysis on the ROM and add provable error bounds to recover a certified over-approximation of the original PDE reachable set.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports explicit trajectory-error bounds between the PDE, FEM model, and ROM, and demonstrates the framework on the Allen--Cahn and logistic growth PDEs.
watch_item: The guarantees depend on the quality of the FEM/POD approximations and may become costly or conservative for strongly nonlinear dynamics or high-dimensional parameter sets.
paper_title: Certified Reachable Sets for Nonlinear Reaction--Diffusion Systems
